findings.title: The required smoke check depends on an undefined reverted spec and non-repeatable LLM evidence

source_refs.value: Run a smoke check on the spec used as the source for this brief (`tasks/builds/v1-freeze-final-hardening/spec.md` in the consuming repo). Success criterion: at least 4 of 6 new Hunt Targets fire on their source finding when invoked against an artificially-reverted version of the spec that still contains the original gaps
Any new Hunt Target that does NOT surface its source finding on the reverted-spec smoke check is logged in §8 Decision log and reviewed before the apply lands.

findings.rationale: The rollout gate is normative, but it does not identify the exact reverted artifact, command, model/mode invocation, or expected machine-readable output. Implementers in the framework repo can neither reproduce the same reverted source spec nor prove the 4-of-6 criterion deterministically, especially because the named source file lives in a consuming repo. This can either block the PR for lack of evidence or let different operators accept incompatible smoke results.
findings.acceptance_check: Add a committed fixture such as `scripts/__fixtures__/chatgpt-spec-review/v1-freeze-final-hardening-reverted.spec.md` plus an exact smoke command in §6.1 step 7; verify with `test -f scripts/__fixtures__/chatgpt-spec-review/v1-freeze-final-hardening-reverted.spec.md` and `grep -n "v1-freeze-final-hardening-reverted.spec.md" <brief-file>`.
